text:
Context
- Feature: Redwood Console > Optimize > Suggestions (beta) for batching/caching recommendations.
- Goal: closed-loop measurement of usefulness + trust: impressions → expand → dismiss → apply click. Also capture basic diagnostics for empty/error states without collecting customer content.
- This doc proposes a v0 event taxonomy aligned to the funnel dashboard spec and the Suggestions API contract (stable suggestion IDs).

Principles / requirements
1) No customer content
- Do NOT include: prompt text, completion text, request/response bodies, raw config payloads, or any user-entered notes.
- Do NOT include: IP address, email, name, or free-form text fields.
- Route identifiers: use Redwood route_id and/or route_name if already considered non-sensitive within the org boundary; if route_name may contain customer data, prefer route_id only.

2) Stable join keys for analysis
- sugge...

title: Suggestions API performance budget (beta) — latency, payload, rate limits, caching

text:
Doc intent
- Capture performance + scale budgets for the Console “Optimization suggestions” beta API.
- This is a constraints doc for engineering: if we can’t meet these numbers, Console UX (table + drawer) will feel sluggish and we’ll get pageload regressions.

Scope
- Suggestions list endpoint + (optional) details endpoint.
- Beta scope = batching + caching suggestions only.
- Org/project scoped, RBAC enforced. No cross-tenant access.

Non-goals
- Real-time per-request inference; suggestions are batch computed and served from storage.
- Guaranteeing “savings accuracy” (handled separately); this doc is about runtime performance and availability.

Assumptions / traffic model (beta)
- Primary UI path: Console loads Suggestions view once per user session; user may sort/filter/paginate; opens 0–5 drawers.
- Expected “active beta orgs”: 10–30 orgs initially, scaling to 100–200 orgs during...

text:
Context
- This doc enumerates the minimum telemetry signals required to generate Optimization Suggestions (beta scope: batching + caching only) with (a) estimated cost savings ($/day, %), (b) estimated performance impact (p50/p95 latency deltas), and (c) risk label inputs (Low/Medium/High) that are explainable.
- Audience: Telemetry/Observability + Optimize pipeline engineers + Console backend.
- Output consumer: Optimize Suggestions Service compute job + Suggestions API + Console UI.

Non-goals (for this doc)
- UI funnel events (impression/expand/dismiss/apply). Those are specified elsewhere (see ENG-19402).
- Full “quality regression” eval data (prompt sets, output scoring). We only need enough proxy signals for performance/reliability risk in v0.

Key definitions (align on naming)
- Org / Project: tenant and sub-tenant boundary. All aggregates MUST respect these boundaries.
- Route...

text:
Context
- We’re rolling out tiered SLOs + error budgets for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity). For those to be credible, tier labeling must be:
 (1) set once (authoritative),
 (2) propagated end-to-end across gateway → routing/control plane → model serving,
 (3) present on the right metrics/logs/traces used for SLIs, and
 (4) safe from cardinality blow-ups.
- This doc is the concrete implementation plan for “tier tagging” in telemetry. It’s intended as the working plan used by Telemetry + Gateway + Routing to land ENG-4922 and unblock dashboards/alerts.

Goals
1) Canonical tags: define the minimal label set required to compute SLIs for availability, latency, and streaming reliability by tier/region/endpoint class.
2) Authoritative source: tier tag set at ingress (API gateway), not “inferred later” in routing.
3) Propagation: tier tag carried in request context and appli...

text:
Context
- SIEM-ready audit export beta: exporter service pushes audit events to Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs intake, and S3 delivery.
- This doc is a spreadsheet-like inventory of proposed metrics and dashboard panels.
- Primary goals:
 1) measure export lag + delivery success, per adapter type
 2) surface customer-impacting failures (auth, throttling, misconfig)
 3) keep label/cardinality safe for shared hosted and dedicated environments

Metric conventions (proposed)
- Prefix: audit_exporter_
- Use Prometheus-style suffixes: _total counters, _seconds for time, _bytes, _ratio, _info for build/version.
- Avoid embedding identifiers in metric names.

Labeling + cardinality rules (beta)
- Allowed low-card labels:
 - region (e.g., us-east-1, eu-west-1)
 - deployment_mode (hosted|dedicated) [NOTE: private out of scope for beta]
 - adapter (s3|splunk_hec|datadog_logs)
 - outcome (success|error...

text:
Context
- We added experiment attribution dimensions to request context + telemetry: {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id} (plus model_id, region) per ADR-048.
- Console “Results” view and guardrails are driven off rollup tables keyed by {route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, window_start}.
- During beta we had a schema change + partial rollout (feature flags + region ramp). As a result:
 - historical windows (and even some recent windows during rollout) are missing experiment_id/variant_id
 - Console results appear stale or “no data” for experiments created before the schema flip
 - support tickets are increasingly about “results not updating” when the underlying traffic clearly existed

Goal
Backfill the minimal set of metrics needed by Console route experiment dashboards/guardrails for a bounded time window after the schema change.

Non-goals (for this backfill)
- Not attempting a f...

text:
Last updated: 2025-03-28 (Logan)
Scope: staging only. Goal is to quantify “attribution drop rate” for route-level experiments and identify root causes + fixes before wider beta.

---
Definitions

Attribution fields (expected on request span + metrics events):
- route_id (normalized template)
- experiment_id
- variant_id
- model_id
- region

Attribution drop rate (ADR for this doc; not to be confused with Architecture Decision Record):
- For a given population of requests that SHOULD be eligible for experiment attribution (i.e., route has an active experiment AND request passed experiment evaluation), the % of request records in telemetry that are missing experiment_id OR variant_id.
- Computation uses OTel spans as source of truth for “request seen”; results table uses aggregator view (analytics rollup) for consistency with Console.

Eligibility notes:
- Some traffic is legitimately n...

title: Experiment metrics taxonomy (working notes) — route-level A/B testing

- Route-level experiments need first-class metrics for guardrails + results views.

 (1) what we emit (OTel spans/metrics + logs)
 (2) what we aggregate (rollups for Console)
 (3) what we alert on (SRE guardrails)
- We are optimizing for: correctness, stable definiti...
text:
Context
- Route-level experiments need first-class metrics for guardrails + results views.
- This doc is a working taxonomy to align:
 (1) what we emit (OTel spans/metrics + logs)
 (2) what we aggregate (rollups for Console)
 (3) what we alert on (SRE guardrails)
- We are optimizing for: correctness, stable definitions, and avoiding metric cardinality blow-ups.

Non-goals (for now)
- No full stat-sig engine (Console will show deltas + guardrail status only).
- No customer custom metrics ingestion.
- No per-user breakdowns beyond deterministic assignment.

1) Canonical attribution dimensions (labels/attributes)
We need a single canonical set of dimensions across telemetry + rollups.

Required dimensions (request-scoped)
- route_id (normalized, templated route; NOT raw path)
- experiment_id (stable UUID / opaque id)
- variant_id (stable id within experiment config; do not use display na...

text:
Meeting: Internal Unit Economics Dashboard kickoff (v1)
Date/time: 2025-01-15 10:00–11:00 PT
Location: Zoom
Facilitator: Talia Benson (PgM)
Notes: Logan Wright

Attendees
- Product: Marissa Cole (Console & Rollouts), Aditya Rao (Optimize) [partial]
- Eng (Telemetry/Analytics): Logan Wright, Nadia Rahman, Mei Lin, Caleb Johnson
- Eng (Console/Tooling): Chloe Martin, Felix Schneider [partial], Nora Ahmed [partial]
- SRE/Obs: Sean Gallagher, Allison Grant
- Finance: Rishi Malhotra (FP&A), Laura Bennett (CFO) [first 20 mins]

Why we’re here (problem statement)
- Today we can answer “what’s our infra spend?” and “what’s our token volume?” but not reliably “what is blended $/1M output tokens by model/region/tier and why did it change?”
- Goal is a finance-aligned metric definition + dependable pipeline + internal Console UI for drilldowns.
- v1 is internal-only; used for exec reporting + co...
